title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLXIX. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
There was an old man of Tobago,
        Who lived on rice, gruel, and sago;
            Till, much to his bliss,
            His physician said this - 
        "To a leg, sir, of mutton you may go."
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCLXIX. Gaffers And Gammers." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
